semanticCore: Power, establishment, place, authority, or egg
primaryMeaning: To establish or place in authority
quranicRelevance: Establishing in power/authority (Tamkeen - 'makkanna lahum'), and status/position (Makanah - 'ala makanatikum').

keyDerivations.meaning: To establish, settle, empower, or give authority to
Place, location, status, position, or standard
Established, secure, honored, or in high standing

figurativeOrRareUsages: عَلَى مَكَانَتِكُمْ: According to your position/manner.
فِي قَرَارٍ مَكِينٍ: In a secure place/lodging (the womb).
discardedNoise: The egg of a lizard (مَكْن) - original physical base.
summaryEn: The root denotes power and establishment, portraying the political/social empowerment (Tamkeen) of believers and secure physiological placing (Makeen).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على التمكين والقدرة، واصفاً التمكين المجتمعي والسياسي للمؤمنين (مكّنا)، والمستقر البدني الحصين للجنين (قرار مكين).
rawText: أَمْكَنَهُ مِنْ شَىْءِ 4 He made him to have a thing within his power, or reach: enabled him to do, reach, get, or obtain, a thing. See 2. === أَمْكَنَهُ It was within his power, or reach; was possible, or practicable, to him. === أَمْكَنَهُ It became easy to him. (Msb.) It (an object of the chase) offered him an opportunity to shoot it or capture it; or became within his power, or reach. === أَمْكِنِى, said to a woman, [meaning Empower thou; i. e. grant thou access;] occurs in a poem. (S, art. عرض.) === أَمْكَنَتْهُ She granted him attainment. اِسْتَمْكَنَ 10 : see 5. === He, or it, was, or became, firm. It seems sometimes to mean It (a plant) took firm root. تَمَكَّنَ 5 i. q. اِسْتَقَرَّ: (Msb, art. قر:) it is very often used in this sense, as meaning He, or it, settled; became fixed, or established; it became fixed, or steady, in its place; when said of a man, particularly implying in authority and power: see قَرَّ. === تَمَكَّنَ مِنْ شَىْءٍ, and 🡻اِسْتَمْكَنَ, He became possessed of mastery, or dominion, or ascendancy, or authority, and power, over a thing; he was able to avail himself of it: [he was, or became, within reach of him, or it.] (Msb.) === تَمَكَّنَ مِنْهُ He assumed authority over him. جَلَسَ مُتَمَكِّنًا He sat in a firm, or settled, posture; as when one sits cross-legged. مَكَانَةٌ Greatness, and high rank or standing. in the estimation of the Sultán: (Msb:) an honourable place in the estimation of a king. (K.) مَكِنَةٌ i. q. تَمَكُّنُ. (Sh, TA.) === النَّاسُ عَلَى مَكِنَاتِهِمْ means على مَقَارِّهِمْ. (IAar, TA.) مَكَّانُ : see مَصَّانٌ in art. مص. مَكَّنَهُ 2 He gave him a place: (Jel, vi. 6:) he assigned him a place, and settled, or established, him. (Bd, ibid, where see more.) You say also, مَكَّنَ لَهُ فِى مَنْزِلٍ [He assigned, or gave, him a place in an abode]. (S in art. بوأ.) === مَكَّنَهُ مِنْ شَىْءٍ, and 🡻أَمْكَنَهُ, He made him to have mastery, or dominion, or ascendancy, or authority, and power, over a thing; (Msb;) put it in his power. === مَكَّنَهُ مِنَ الشَّىْءِ, and 🡻أَمْكَنَمِنْهُ, He empowered him, enabled him, or rendered him able, to do the thing: he enabled him to have the thing within his power. Ex. 🡻أَمْكَنَيَدَيْهِ مِنْ رُكْبَتَيْهِ He enabled his hands to take and grasp his knees. from a trad. (Mgh.) مَكْنَانٌ : see رَيِّحَةٌ. مُكْنَةٌ , (Msb, TA,) with damm, (TA,) Power; (Msb, * TA;) ability; (TA;) strength. (Msb.)
